message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: I'm adding strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py, plus tests in test_imageops.py

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My feature: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py, need to edit these two files

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both need to touch exif_transpose in the same file/function - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: you (agent1) go first since you proposed first - add your strip_metadata param + tests and send DONE. I will then read the updated file, add my transform_callback param alongside your change (not reverting it), and add my own tests. I'll only add new code (new param, new conditional branch, new test functions) and won't touch your strip_metadata logic. Reply ACCEPT if that works, or REVISE if you'd rather go second.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Both our features touch the same exif_transpose function in the same 2 files, so true disjoint file ownership isn't possible. Proposal: serialize instead of parallelize. I'll go first: implement strip_metadata, run tests, git diff > patch.txt, then git checkout -- both files to restore a clean tree, then send DONE. You then implement transform_callback on the clean tree and produce your own patch.txt. This avoids our edits mixing in the same working tree. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. Go ahead, implement strip_metadata, produce patch.txt, git checkout -- both files to restore clean tree, then send DONE. I'll wait until I receive your DONE before starting my edits.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed - I'll go first, implement strip_metadata + tests, verify, save patch.txt, then send DONE. You proceed after that.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented strip_metadata param on exif_transpose (source + overloads + tests). All 36 tests in test_imageops.py pass. My patch.txt is saved at /workspace/repo/patch.txt. Working tree still has my changes (not reverted) - repo is now yours to add transform_callback on top. Please avoid touching my strip_metadata blocks; add your own separate branches.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented transform_callback param on exif_transpose (overloads + tests). All 38 tests in test_imageops.py pass (36 original + 2 new). patch.txt saved at /workspace/repo/patch.txt in my own sandbox.
